Building and construction require technical calculations: concrete, steel, bricks, plaster, waterproofing. Industry standards ensure proper processes.

## How It's Calculated

Calculate the number of rolls needed based on square meters (1 roll = 10 m²) · Free and instant.

This calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you to unusually high or low values.

## Important Notes

This calculation is a reference tool only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult with a qualified professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: What is a standard asphalt membrane roll size?
faq.a: A standard roll is 1 m × 10 m = 10 m². Thickness varies between 3, 4, or 5 mm.

faq.q: How much overlap do I need?
faq.a: Plan for 10 cm overlaps. This adds 10-15% to your total square meters.
Professional torch application or adhesive installation. Torch method is more common for durability.

faq.q: Should I use a two-layer system?
faq.a: Two layers in opposite directions provide better durability and weather resistance.
Aluminum or gravel protects from UV damage. Unprotected membranes typically last 5-7 years.
